title: Red eye
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped red-eye, discharge, itching, burning, grittiness, watering, laterality, exposure, and course fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

presenting_history.text: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped contact-lens red-eye, pain, photophobia, discharge, blurred vision, and corneal concern fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Microbial keratitis (bacterial, fungal)

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped visual change, severe pain, photophobia, trauma, headache with nausea, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, and severe foreign-body warning fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

red_flags.text: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.
red_flags.rationale: Supports emergency-context review; no additional legacy items are mapped from this section.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Microbial keratitis (bacterial, fungal)

exact_section.heading: Patient summary — sight-threatening emergency and referral

referral.text: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-led eye care, contact-lens, return, review, referral, and medication-plan documentation without doses.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

follow_up.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped history questions and clinician-assessed visual acuity, conjunctiva, discharge, cornea, and pupil documentation.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

source_limitations: Broad bloodshot or ocular-hyperaemia synonyms, eyelid and movement examination, fluorescein, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, previous records, and exact medication choices remain unsupported.
Conjunctivitis, keratitis, glaucoma, or other diagnosis must remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
